title: angophora × clelandii
intro: Angophora × clelandii, also known as the Cleland's angophora or rough-barked apple, is a unique plant species that belongs to the myrtle family (Myrtaceae) and is a natural hybrid of Angophora costata and Angophora subvelutina. The plant is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which has been used to treat various health issues. The essential oil extracted from Angophora × clelandii has been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including the relief of pain, reduction of inflammation, and promotion of wound healing. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
